name: Maternal minute ventilation at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015 ERS Breathe review: minute ventilation rises ~40% in pregnancy, driven primarily by tidal-volume increase. Progesterone-mediated.
notes: Resting minute ventilation at term; increased ~30-50% above non-pregnant baseline.

name: Pregnancy P50 shift
tier_rationale: Tier B from Kelman 1966 J Appl Physiol: the original digital subroutine for O2-Hb saturation, including temperature and pH shifts. Used as the foundational reference for pregnancy P50 shifts attributable to 2,3-DPG and pH changes.
notes: Rightward shift in maternal oxyhaemoglobin P50 in pregnancy, attributed to elevated 2,3-BPG. Caveats: Reported shift varies by methodology and 2,3-BPG measurement; a 2-4 mmHg range is most commonly cited.

name: Maternal arterial PCO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier A from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015 ERS Breathe review: arterial PaCO2 'plateaus around 32 mmHg' at term, consistent with progesterone-driven hyperventilation. Multiple independent cohorts referenced in the masterclass review.
notes: PaCO2 at term, post-hyperventilation plateau. Updated from the original v0.2 value of 30 mmHg to 32 mmHg to match the value reported in LoMauro & Aliverti 2015. The original 30 was likely an extraction error; the original 'high' bound (32) matched the paper's plateau value.

name: Maternal arterial PO2 at term
tier_rationale: Tier A from LoMauro & Aliverti 2015 ERS Breathe review: PaO2 'reaches 101-104 mmHg in the third trimester' as a compensatory rise after PaCO2 falls. Range tightened to match the third-trimester window reported.
notes: PaO2 at term, post-hyperventilation. Range tightened from 98-108 (which spans first- and third-trimester) to 101-105, matching the third-trimester range reported by LoMauro 2015.

name: Maternal tidal volume at term
tier_rationale: Tier B from Crapo 1996 Clinical Obstetrics and Gynecology: review of cardiopulmonary physiology in pregnancy. Tidal volume rises ~200 mL above non-pregnant baseline at term.
